Below it one row is marked "Coming soon" — Claude; its toggle is off and can't be clicked.

features.title: Connecting Google Calendar
features.body: On the Google Calendar page, choose how far back to start syncing ("Sync from date"). The default is one year back, and you can't go further than that. Click "Connect Google Calendar", and Google's permissions screen will open. Once you approve, events will start coming into the app automatically.

features.title: Syncing and Disconnecting the Calendar
features.body: When the calendar is connected, you can see when it was last synced. The "Sync now" button pulls in events again and tells you how many were added and how many were removed. The "Disconnect" button requires a second click to confirm ("Are you sure? Disconnect").

features.title: Synced Events and How They're Identified
features.body: Under "Synced events" you can see your events, grouped by clients, projects, leads, groups, and "Unidentified". Each event is marked "Identified" or "Unidentified" based on its association, with the date, time, and session length shown alongside.

features.title: Manually Associating an Event
features.body: For any event you can click "+ Associate with…", choose what to link it to (client, project, lead, or group), and then select the specific record from the list. You can also remove an existing association with the X on its tag. Associations you set manually are saved and won't be overwritten by future syncs.

features.title: Connecting Invoicing (Green Invoice / SUMIT)
features.body: On the Invoicing page, choose a provider — "Green Invoice" or "SUMIT" — and paste in your credentials. For Green Invoice: API Key and Secret (the key and secret from your service account). For SUMIT: Company ID and API Key. The "How do I get these?" button walks you through exactly where to find them in each service.

features.title: Testing, Disconnecting, and Reconnecting
features.body: After connecting, the status shows "Connected" with the provider name. "Test connection" verifies with the service that your credentials are still valid, and "Disconnect" requires a second click. If the key was revoked or replaced, you'll see "Reconnection required" with a "Reconnect" button to enter new credentials.

features.title: Auto-Importing Revenue
features.body: When invoicing is connected, you can turn on the "Auto-import revenue?" checkbox. When it's on, the system detects invoices you've issued in your invoicing software and asks whether to record them as revenue here too. It won't record anything without your confirmation.

features.title: Pending Imports
features.body: Below the invoicing card there's a "Pending imports" list — invoices you've issued in the external service that are waiting for your approval. Each row shows the document type and number, amount, client name, a "View" link to the document (if available), and a confirmation button ("Import as revenue", requires two clicks) or a reject button. If it looks like a duplicate of an existing transaction, a soft warning appears before you confirm.

tips: For Google Calendar, if you choose an earlier start date before connecting, you'll pull in a longer history of events. After connecting, you can't extend the range further back without disconnecting and reconnecting (and even then, no more than one year back).
Events the system didn't recognize ("Unidentified") are worth associating manually — with a client, project, lead, or group. The association is saved and won't be overwritten by future syncs.
Not sure if your invoicing credentials are still valid? Click "Test connection" on the Invoicing page. It checks with the service without disconnecting anything.
The Secret for Green Invoice is only shown once, when you create the key. Copy it right away, before you close the window.
If you've already recorded revenue manually and it also appears in "Pending imports" with a "Possible duplicate" label — reject the import so it doesn't get recorded twice.
For SUMIT you need the private API key, not the public one. Otherwise the connection will fail.

faq.q: I connected Google Calendar but the events aren't showing up?
faq.a: Go to the Google Calendar page and click "Sync now". If events still don't appear, they may be outside the date range you chose when connecting — disconnect and reconnect with an earlier start date.

faq.q: Can I connect both Green Invoice and SUMIT at the same time?
faq.a: No. The Invoicing page supports one active provider at a time, and connecting a different provider replaces the previous one. Choose the service you work with.

faq.q: Can I issue an invoice to a client from this screen?
faq.a: Not directly. This screen is only for connecting the service. To issue an invoice or receipt, go to a revenue transaction in the Finance screen — the connection is what enables that option there.

faq.q: What do I do when it says "Reconnection required"?
faq.a: It means the service rejected the credentials you saved — they were probably revoked or replaced. Click "Reconnect", enter new credentials, and click connect.

faq.q: The "Auto-import revenue?" checkbox — does it record revenue without asking me?
faq.a: No. When it's on, it detects invoices you've issued in your invoicing software and adds them to "Pending imports". But they're only recorded as revenue after you confirm each one yourself.

faq.q: Where are the credentials I entered stored?
faq.a: Your credentials are stored on our servers and are linked only to your account. The browser doesn't read them back, and there's no way to view them again from the screen — not even for you.

settings.title: Settings
settings.intro: Settings is where you customize the app to fit your workflow. It's divided into nine sections — from your profile and preferred form of address, through widgets, statuses, daily questions and design, to export, import and account deletion.

features.title: How the screen is organized — nine collapsible sections
features.body: Settings are divided into sections that open one at a time: Profile, Widgets & Display, Clients & Statuses, Payments & Currency, Daily Questions, Lead Settings, Design, Data, and About. All sections start collapsed. When you open one, the previous one closes.

features.title: Profile — name, form of address and profession
features.body: Fill in your full name, choose your preferred form of address (feminine, masculine or neutral), and select your profession from the available options (therapist, coach, facilitator, teacher, instructor, other). Choosing "Other" opens a free-text field — "What do you do?" — for your own description. Your form of address changes the wording throughout the entire app.

features.title: Auto-save in Profile
features.body: There is no Save button here. Your name and free-text field are saved as soon as you click out of the field, and a small "· Saved" note appears next to them. Even if you close the section before clicking out — what you typed is saved. Choosing a form of address or profession is saved immediately on click.

features.title: Widgets & Display — general display settings
features.body: At the top of this section are three controls that affect all cards on the Home screen: "Card Style" (blurred, flat or outlined), "Text Weight" (normal or bold) and "Density" (compact, normal or spacious).

features.title: Managing Home widgets
features.body: Below the general controls is a collapsible section called "Widgets". There you can toggle each widget on or off and reorder them by dragging or using the arrow buttons. Each active widget gets its own density control (global, compact, normal or spacious), and some have a "Compact" mode as well. The list includes the daily quote, overview ring, Daily Check-In, quick add / goal update, alerts, upcoming reminders, next tasks and status cards.

features.title: Clients & Statuses — custom sub-statuses
features.body: Here you add and delete sub-statuses for each client group (active, in-between, former, no status). Type a name and press the plus button or Enter. If you delete a sub-status that has clients in it, a window opens letting you move them to another sub-status or leave them without one. You can also undo if you make a mistake.

features.title: Payments & Currency
features.body: Choose your currency (₪ Shekel, $ Dollar or € Euro), date format (DD/MM/YY, MM/DD/YY or YYYY-MM-DD), time format (24-hour or 12h AM/PM), and which day the week starts on (Sunday or Monday). The currency you choose changes how amounts are displayed throughout the app.

features.title: Daily Questions — adding, disabling and deleting
features.body: You start with no questions and add them yourself via "Add Question". You can choose from a ready-made template (mood, energy, sleep, focus, nutrition, movement, stillness), or write your own with free text, answer type (scale 1–10 or yes/no) and an icon. Each question can be disabled, enabled or deleted. A question linked to a goal is marked with a target icon.

features.title: Question schedule
features.body: Clicking the schedule label on a question opens an editor with three options: "Every day", "Specific days" (choose days of the week) and "Every X days" (every 2 to 30 days). This way a question can appear only on the days you choose. Click "Save" to confirm, or "Cancel" to exit without changes.

features.title: Daily reminder for questions
features.body: At the bottom of the Questions section, under "Daily Reminder", you can turn on a reminder and set a time. If you haven't answered by that time, a gentle nudge appears in the Daily Check-In widget on the Home screen. The time field only appears when the reminder is on.

features.title: Lead Settings — lead sources and sub-statuses
features.body: Under "Lead Sources" you add new sources with a color and delete existing ones. Under "Sub-Statuses" you manage the sub-statuses for the lead board columns (in progress, converted, not relevant) — also with colors, and the same delete-with-reassign flow as for clients.

features.title: Design — light/dark mode and text size
features.body: Here you choose light/dark mode (day or night) and text size (small, normal or large). Text size only changes the font, not the screen layout. To change your form of address, go to the Profile section, not here.

features.title: Data — summary, export and import
features.body: At the top of this section you can see how many of each item you have (clients, transactions, leads, tasks, projects, categories). "Export Data" opens one dialog: either an Excel file with a sheet for each type, or a separate export of transactions / clients / projects as a CSV. "Import from File" accepts CSV, TSV and Excel files — including multiple files and sheets at once — auto-detects columns, and lets you review and adjust the mapping before anything is imported.

features.title: Exporting sensitive data (optional)
features.body: The export dialog has checkboxes for sensitive data — sessions, goals, daily answers and reflections. They are off by default and reset every time you open the dialog. Checking a box adds more sheets to the Excel file, and the data exports as plain text — so keep the file somewhere safe.

features.title: Restart the onboarding flow
features.body: This restarts the onboarding wizard from the beginning — useful if you skipped through it. It is non-destructive: the wizard steps reset, but everything you already created (clients, projects, etc.) stays. You'll see a short confirmation before proceeding.

features.title: Reset account — delete all data
features.body: "Delete all data and start over" removes everything in your account (clients, projects, groups, leads, transactions and recurring expenses, categories, tasks, goals, questions and answers, sessions, notes, reminders, statuses and sources) and restarts the onboarding. You need to confirm twice — first a warning, then type the word "delete". This cannot be undone.

features.title: Delete account permanently
features.body: "Delete account permanently" marks your entire account for deletion — including your login itself, not just the data. You need to confirm twice and type "delete account". It does not happen immediately: a 30-day grace period begins during which you can change your mind and cancel on any login. Only after that grace period is everything permanently deleted.

features.title: About — three tabs
features.body: The "About" section has three tabs: "About" (what the app is, its principles, version and credits) with links to the Privacy Policy, Terms of Use and Data Processing Agreement; "Guide" — the full explanation for every screen (the same help that appears in the ? button); and "FAQ".

tips: It's worth setting your form of address in the Profile section first — it changes the wording throughout the entire app, including text on other screens.
Your profile saves automatically: just click out of the field (or close the section) and the change is captured. There is no Save button.
It's worth exporting all your data to Excel at least once a month as a backup. The client and project files are saved in a format that can be re-imported.

Before deleting a sub-status that has clients or leads in it — choose in the dialog where to move them, so you don't leave records without a label. And even if you make a mistake, there's an undo option.
Want a daily question that only appears on weekdays? Open the question's schedule, choose "Specific days" and check the days.
Don't confuse "Reset account" (deletes data, you type "delete") with "Delete account permanently" (deletes your login too, you type "delete account", with 30 days to change your mind).

faq.q: I changed my name or profession and didn't see a Save button — was it saved?
faq.a: Yes. There is no Save button in Profile. Your name and free-text field are saved as soon as you click out of the field (and even if you close the section mid-edit), and selecting a form of address or profession saves immediately on click. A "· Saved" note appears next to a field that has been saved.

faq.q: Why don't I have any daily questions in the Questions section?
faq.a: That's expected — the app doesn't set up questions in advance. Click "Add Question" and choose a ready-made template or create your own. Once you've added questions, you can set a schedule for each one and enable or disable them.

faq.q: What's the difference between "Reset account" and "Delete account permanently"?
faq.a: Resetting your account deletes all your data and restarts the onboarding, but your login stays. Deleting your account marks your login itself for deletion — with a 30-day grace period to cancel before everything is permanently deleted. Both actions require double confirmation and typing a different word ("delete" vs. "delete account").

faq.q: If I restart the onboarding, will I lose the data I already entered?
faq.a: No. "Restart onboarding" only resets the wizard steps to the beginning. The clients, projects and data you already created stay. If you want to delete the data itself, that's "Reset account" in the danger section.

faq.q: Does the export include my sensitive sessions and reflections?
faq.a: Only if you check the boxes. The export dialog has checkboxes for sensitive data (sessions, goals, daily answers, reflections), and they are off by default. When you check them, they are added to the Excel file as plain text — so keep the file somewhere safe.

faq.q: Where do I change dark mode or text size?
faq.a: In the "Design" section. There you choose light/dark mode and text size (small, normal, large). Text size only changes the font, not the screen layout. To change your form of address (masculine/feminine/neutral), go to the Profile section instead.

trash.title: Trash
trash.intro: Here you can view and restore items you've deleted — clients, transactions, sessions, tasks, leads, goals and more — before they're gone for good.

features.title: Every deletion here is still reversible
features.body: Almost every deletion in the app doesn't remove the item immediately — it moves here, to the Trash. At the top you can see how many items are here ("N items"), alongside the reminder "Kept for 30 days" and the message "Every deletion here is still reversible."

features.title: Items are organized by type
features.body: Deleted items are grouped by type, and each group shows an icon, name and item count. Types include: clients, projects, groups, tasks, leads, lead sources, lead sub-statuses, transactions, expense categories, recurring templates, sessions, reminders, goals, goal metrics, goal entries, daily questions and daily answers.

features.title: Restoring an item
features.body: Every item has a "Restore" button. Clicking it returns the item to the screen it was deleted from, with all its data. During the operation the button shows "Restoring…". You don't need to select an item first — the button acts directly on that row.

features.title: When each item was deleted
features.body: Below each item's name it shows "Deleted" and how long ago that happened (e.g. "Deleted 3 days ago"). This makes it easy to know how much time is left before it disappears from the Trash.

features.title: Kept for 30 days
features.body: The Trash only shows items deleted in the last 30 days. An item deleted more than 30 days ago no longer appears here and is on its way to being permanently removed from the system — so restore it in time.

features.title: Empty trash
features.body: When there's nothing to restore, you'll see a message that "The trash is empty", with a note that anything you delete in the future will wait here for 30 days. There's nothing to do here — it simply means nothing has been deleted recently.

tips: Restoring returns the item exactly to the place it was deleted from (clients, transactions, goals, etc.) with all its data. No need to re-enter anything.

If you just deleted something by mistake, it's faster to click "Undo" on the brief notification that pops up after the deletion (for about 6 seconds). The Trash is your safety net for when that notification is already gone.
Items you want to keep — restore them within 30 days of deletion. After that they disappear from the Trash and cannot be recovered.
Before deleting your account or doing a major cleanup, back up your data via Settings → Data → Export Data.

faq.q: How do I permanently delete an item from the Trash?
faq.a: There is no permanent-delete button here — the only action is "Restore". Items are permanently deleted automatically after 30 days, and there's no way to speed that up manually from here.

faq.q: I can't find an item I deleted?
faq.a: More than 30 days may have passed since the deletion, in which case it's no longer shown in the Trash. Another possibility: the item is restored together with its parent (like group members), or one of the groups failed to load — in which case an error message appears at the top.

faq.q: What happens when an item is restored?
faq.a: It returns immediately to the screen it was deleted from, with all its data, and disappears from the Trash. The update is instant visually, and syncs to the server in the background. If the server returns an error, the Trash reloads so what you see stays accurate.

faq.q: Does everything deleted in the app end up here?
faq.a: Almost every soft deletion lands in the Trash — clients, transactions, sessions, tasks, leads, goals and more. Some related items (like group members) are restored through their parent item, so they don't appear here separately.

faq.q: There's an error message at the top — what does that mean?
faq.a: It means one of the item groups failed to load. The other groups still display — one failed load doesn't empty the whole Trash. Try refreshing the page, and if the error persists, contact support.

admin.title: Admin Console
admin.intro: Management tools for the system owner: users, feedback and analytics.

features.title: User management
features.body: View all registered users, grant admin permissions, and delete accounts.

features.title: Feedback
features.body: All feedback submitted via the "Talk to us" button — read and respond.

features.title: Analytics
features.body: Usage data: number of active users, popular screens, and more.

items.q: I forgot my password — how do I reset it?
items.a: On the login screen, click "Forgot password?". Enter your email address and click "Send link". If the address is registered with us, we'll send a link to it. The link takes you to the "Choose a new password" screen, where you pick a new password (at least 6 characters) and confirm it.

items.q: How do I change my password when I'm already logged in?
items.a: Use the same "Choose a new password" screen. You can reach it via "Forgot password?" on the login screen, or via the reset link sent to your email.

items.q: How do I change my email address?
items.a: Currently, changing your account email address is only possible by contacting support.

items.q: I signed in with Google — do I need a password too?
items.a: No. With Google you don't need a password. Just click "Sign in with Google" each time.

items.q: Do I need to agree to anything when signing up?
items.a: Yes. When signing up (both with email and with Google) you check two boxes: one to accept the Privacy Policy and Data Processing Agreement, and one to accept the Terms of Use. There's also an optional box for marketing emails — it's not required and you can uncheck it at any time.

items.q: Who can see my data?
items.a: Only you. Each account is completely separate, and only you can see your account's data. The connection to the server is secure. We do not sell your data or share it with anyone.

items.q: Are sensitive fields encrypted in the database?
items.a: Notes, session summaries and reflections are stored on our end as plain text. What protects them: only you access your account, and the connection is secure. If you used early beta versions, some fields may still be stored in encrypted form from the past — they are decrypted automatically for you.

items.q: What happens to my Green Invoice / SUMIT API keys?
items.a: Your connection credentials (the identifier and key) are stored on our server only. They are never shown in the browser — not even to you. The system uses them only when it issues an invoice or pulls income on your behalf.

items.q: How do I back up my data?
items.a: Settings → Data → Export Data. You can download all your data in a single Excel file (one sheet per type: transactions, clients, projects, leads, tasks, categories). You can also export clients, transactions or projects separately as CSV files.

items.q: Can I include sensitive data (sessions, goals, daily answers, reflections) in the export?
items.a: Yes. The export dialog has checkboxes for this, and they are off by default. They add extra sheets to the full Excel file. Sensitive data exports as plain text, so keep the file somewhere safe.

items.q: How do I import data from Excel / Google Sheets?
items.a: Settings → Data → Import from File (CSV / Excel). You can upload CSV, TSV and Excel files (xlsx/xls), including files with multiple sheets. The system auto-detects the columns, and you can manually correct the mapping before importing.

items.q: How do I delete my account?
items.a: Settings → Data → Delete account permanently. This deletes the entire account, including your login, and requires two confirmations with typed input. After deletion there is a 30-day grace period — during that time you can change your mind and cancel.

items.q: How do I switch to dark mode?
items.a: Open the menu ("More" in the navigation) and click the "Dark mode" toggle (when it's on it's called "Light mode"). You can also go to Settings → Design → Light/Dark Mode.

items.q: How do I make the text larger?
items.a: Settings → Design → Text Size. There are three options: small, normal, large.

items.q: How do I set my preferred form of address (masculine/feminine/neutral)?
items.a: Settings → Profile → Form of Address. Your choice (neutral / feminine / masculine) changes the wording throughout the entire app.

items.q: Can I change the order of screens in the navigation?
items.a: The screen order in navigation is fixed for now. You can customize the Home screen: Settings → Widgets & Display. There you enable and disable widgets, change their order and choose a display style.

items.q: The app is frozen / not loading?
items.a: Refresh the page (F5 on desktop, or pull down on mobile). If it continues — log out and log back in.

items.q: Data I saved isn't showing up?
items.a: Refresh the page. If something is still missing, check that you have an internet connection. If it continues, contact us via "Talk to us" in the menu.

items.q: The + button isn't responding?
items.a: There's probably a dialog or drawer open in the background. Close any open dialogs and try again.

items.q: Which services can I connect?
items.a: Currently: Google Calendar (pulls events from your calendar and identifies sessions with clients), an invoicing service — Green Invoice or SUMIT (issues invoices and receipts and imports income), and WhatsApp (manual sending of messages to clients, with wording you can customise). Claude is marked "Coming soon" and is not yet active.

items.q: How do I get to the Connections screen?
items.a: Via the "Connections" tab in the navigation. The screen shows a list: Google Calendar, Invoicing, and "Coming soon" rows. Each active row opens to its own screen where you connect and manage it.

items.q: How do I connect Google Calendar?
items.a: Connections → Google Calendar. Choose which date to sync from, click "Connect Google Calendar", and approve the permissions in the Google dialog. After that you can sync any time by clicking "Sync Now", and assign events to a client, project, lead or group.

items.q: How do I connect an invoicing service?
items.a: Connections → Invoicing. Choose a provider (Green Invoice or SUMIT), paste your credentials from your account with that service, and click "Connect". The "How do I get these?" button has step-by-step instructions.

items.q: Can I connect both Green Invoice and SUMIT?
items.a: Only one invoicing service at a time. To switch providers, disconnect the current one and connect the new one.

items.q: My invoicing service credentials aren't being accepted?
items.a: Make sure you copied the identifier and key in full, without spaces, and that you're using the private API key (not the public one). After connecting, you can verify with the "Check Connection" button. If the connection suddenly stopped working ("Reconnection required"), the key was probably revoked or replaced — click "Reconnect" and enter it again.

items.q: What does "Auto-import income" mean?
items.a: When this is turned on, the system detects income you've recorded in your invoicing software and shows it to you for approval. Nothing is recorded in your account without your confirmation.

about.tagline: Business OS — at your own pace
about.description: Simplicity was built for therapists, coaches, facilitators, teachers, instructors and consultants in Israel. One place for your entire practice: clients, leads, sessions and calendar, finance, projects, tasks, goals and your own personal process. All in Hebrew, in your preferred form of address (masculine/feminine/neutral), and without the clutter.

principles.title: Privacy first
principles.body: Your data is yours alone. Only you access your account, and the connection to the server is secure. Your invoicing service credentials are stored on our server and are never shown in the browser — not even to you. We do not sell data or rank you.

principles.title: An interface that respects you
principles.body: No noise. No "Premium" pop-ups. No unnecessary alerts. Just what you need to run a practice — at your own pace.

principles.title: Made in Israel
principles.body: Built in Hebrew, with a form of address that fits you (masculine/feminine/neutral), for the community of therapists, coaches and consultants here.
